source: Learning Rsync - rsync password-free authentication login: Setting up password-free authentication for real-time sync

questions.q: Why set up password-free login here?
options: Real-time synchronization (with inotify-tools) runs unattended and cannot stop to type a password
questions.why: Real-time one-way sync needs password-free login so it can run without human input.

questions.q: Password-free login can be set up for...
options: Both the SSH protocol and the rsync protocol
questions.why: Either protocol can achieve password-free authentication.

questions.q: Generate a 2048-bit RSA key pair (SSH password-free login).
questions.why: ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 2048 creates the key pair under ~/.ssh/.

questions.q: For SSH key auth, where does the client's public key go on the server?
Which sshd_config option must be enabled for key authentication?
questions.why: PubkeyAuthentication yes must be set in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.

questions.q: For the rsync protocol, which environment variable supplies the virtual user's password non-interactively?
questions.why: export RSYNC_PASSWORD=<pass> lets the rsync command authenticate without prompting.

questions.q: How do you make RSYNC_PASSWORD persist permanently?
questions.why: Adding the export to /etc/profile makes it take effect for every login.
